See full documentation of [Events and Event Patterns in EventBridge](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eventbridge-and-event-patterns.html) for details. **Note**: The event pattern size is 2048 by default but it is adjustable up to 4096 characters by submitting a service quota increase request. See [Amazon EventBridge quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-quota.html) for details.

force_destroy.description: Used to delete managed rules created by AWS. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: The name of the rule. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random, unique name. Conflicts with `name_prefix`.

name_prefix.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with `name`. **Note**: Due to the length of the generated suffix, must be 38 characters or less.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

role_arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with the role that is used for target invocation.

schedule_expression.description: The scheduling expression. For example, `cron(0 20 * * ? *)` or `rate(5 minutes)`. At least one of `schedule_expression` or `event_pattern` is required. Can only be used on the default event bus. For more information, refer to the AWS documentation [Schedule Expressions for Rules](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/events/ScheduledEvents.html).

state.description: State of the rule. Valid values are `DISABLED`, `ENABLED`, and `ENABLED_WITH_ALL_CLOUDTRAIL_MANAGEMENT_EVENTS`. When state is `ENABLED`, the rule is enabled for all events except those delivered by CloudTrail. To also enable the rule for events delivered by CloudTrail, set `state` to `ENABLED_WITH_ALL_CLOUDTRAIL_MANAGEMENT_EVENTS`. Defaults to `ENABLED`. Conflicts with `is_enabled`.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

appsync_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an AppSync GraphQL API mutation. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

arn.description: - ARN of the SQS queue specified as the target for the dead-letter queue.

array_size.description: The size of the array, if this is an array batch job. Valid values are integers between 2 and 10,000.

assign_public_ip.description: Assign a public IP address to the ENI (Fargate launch type only). Valid values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

base.description: The base value designates how many tasks, at a minimum, to run on the specified capacity provider. Only one capacity provider in a capacity provider strategy can have a base defined. Defaults to `0`.

batch_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an Amazon Batch Job. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

capacity_provider.description: Short name of the capacity provider.

capacity_provider_strategy.description: The capacity provider strategy to use for the task. If a `capacity_provider_strategy` specified, the `launch_type` parameter must be omitted. If no `capacity_provider_strategy` or `launch_type` is specified, the default capacity provider strategy for the cluster is used. Can be one or more. See below.

dead_letter_config.description: Parameters used when you are providing a dead letter config. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

ecs_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke Amazon ECS Task. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

enable_ecs_managed_tags.description: Specifies whether to enable Amazon ECS managed tags for the task.

enable_execute_command.description: Whether or not to enable the execute command functionality for the containers in this task. If true, this enables execute command functionality on all containers in the task.

event_bus_name.description: The name or ARN of the event bus to associate with the rule.

field.description: The field to apply the placement strategy against. For the `spread` placement strategy, valid values are `instanceId` (or `host`, which has the same effect), or any platform or custom attribute that is applied to a container instance, such as `attribute:ecs.availability-zone`. For the `binpack` placement strategy, valid values are `cpu` and `memory`. For the `random` placement strategy, this field is not used. For more information, see [Amazon ECS task placement strategies](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-placement-strategies.html).

force_destroy.description: Used to delete managed rules created by AWS. Defaults to `false`.

graphql_operation.description: Contains the GraphQL mutation to be parsed and executed.

group.description: Specifies an ECS task group for the task. The maximum length is 255 characters.

header_parameters.description: Enables you to specify HTTP headers to add to the request.

http_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an API Gateway REST endpoint. Documented below. A maximum of 1 is allowed.

input.description: Valid JSON text passed to the target. Conflicts with `input_path` and `input_transformer`.

input_path.description: The value of the [JSONPath](http://goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/) that is used for extracting part of the matched event when passing it to the target. Conflicts with `input` and `input_transformer`.

input_paths.description: Key value pairs specified in the form of JSONPath (for example, time = $.time)

input_template.description: Template to customize data sent to the target. Must be valid JSON. To send a string value, the string value must include double quotes. Values must be escaped for both JSON and Terraform, e.g., `"\"Your string goes here.\\nA new line.\""`

input_transformer.description: Parameters used when you are providing a custom input to a target based on certain event data. Conflicts with `input` and `input_path`.

job_attempts.description: The number of times to attempt to retry, if the job fails. Valid values are 1 to 10.

job_definition.description: The ARN or name of the job definition to use if the event target is an AWS Batch job. This job definition must already exist.

job_name.description: The name to use for this execution of the job, if the target is an AWS Batch job.

key.description: Can be either `tag:tag-key` or `InstanceIds`.

kinesis_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an Amazon Kinesis Stream. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

launch_type.description: Specifies the launch type on which your task is running. The launch type that you specify here must match one of the launch type (compatibilities) of the target task. Valid values include: `EC2`, `EXTERNAL`, or `FARGATE`.

maximum_event_age_in_seconds.description: The age in seconds to continue to make retry attempts.

maximum_retry_attempts.description: maximum number of retry attempts to make before the request fails

message_group_id.description: The FIFO message group ID to use as the target.

name.description: Name of parameter to start execution of a SageMaker AI Model Building Pipeline.

network_configuration.description: Use this if the ECS task uses the awsvpc network mode. This specifies the VPC subnets and security groups associated with the task, and whether a public IP address is to be used. Required if `launch_type` is `FARGATE` because the awsvpc mode is required for Fargate tasks.

ordered_placement_strategy.description: An array of placement strategy objects to use for the task. You can specify a maximum of five strategy rules per task.

partition_key_path.description: The JSON path to be extracted from the event and used as the partition key.

path_parameter_values.description: The list of values that correspond sequentially to any path variables in your endpoint ARN (for example `arn:aws:execute-api:us-east-1:123456:myapi/*/POST/pets/*`).

pipeline_parameter_list.description: List of Parameter names and values for SageMaker AI Model Building Pipeline execution.

placement_constraint.description: An array of placement constraint objects to use for the task. You can specify up to 10 constraints per task (including constraints in the task definition and those specified at runtime). See Below.

platform_version.description: Specifies the platform version for the task. Specify only the numeric portion of the platform version, such as `1.1.0`. This is used only if LaunchType is FARGATE. For more information about valid platform versions, see [AWS Fargate Platform Versions](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/platform_versions.html).

propagate_tags.description: Specifies whether to propagate the tags from the task definition to the task. If no value is specified, the tags are not propagated. Tags can only be propagated to the task during task creation. The only valid value is: `TASK_DEFINITION`.

query_string_parameters.description: Represents keys/values of query string parameters that are appended to the invoked endpoint.

redshift_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an Amazon Redshift Statement. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

retry_policy.description: Parameters used when you are providing retry policies. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

role_arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to be used for this target when the rule is triggered. Required if `ecs_target` is used or target in `arn` is EC2 instance, Kinesis data stream, Step Functions state machine, or Event Bus in different account or region.

rule.description: The name of the rule you want to add targets to.

run_command_targets.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke Amazon EC2 Run Command. Documented below. A maximum of 5 are allowed.

sagemaker_pipeline_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an Amazon SageMaker AI Pipeline. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

secrets_manager_arn.description: The name or ARN of the secret that enables access to the database.

security_groups.description: The security groups associated with the task or service. If you do not specify a security group, the default security group for the VPC is used.

sql.description: The SQL statement text to run.

sqs_target.description: Parameters used when you are using the rule to invoke an Amazon SQS Queue. Documented below. A maximum of 1 are allowed.

statement_name.description: The name of the SQL statement.

subnets.description: The subnets associated with the task or service.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to ecs resources.

target_id.description: The unique target assignment ID. If missing, will generate a random, unique id.

task_count.description: The number of tasks to create based on the TaskDefinition. Defaults to `1`.

task_definition_arn.description: The ARN of the task definition to use if the event target is an Amazon ECS cluster.

type.description: Type of constraint. The only valid values at this time are `memberOf` and `distinctInstance`.

value.description: Value of parameter to start execution of a SageMaker AI Model Building Pipeline.

values.description: If Key is `tag:tag-key`, Values is a list of tag values. If Key is `InstanceIds`, Values is a list of Amazon EC2 instance IDs.

weight.description: The weight value designates the relative percentage of the total number of tasks launched that should use the specified capacity provider. The weight value is taken into consideration after the base value, if defined, is satisfied.

with_event.description: Indicates whether to send an event back to EventBridge after the SQL statement runs.

policy_document.description: Text of the account policy. Refer to the [AWS docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/logs/put-account-policy.html) for more information.

policy_type.description: Type of account policy. One of `DATA_PROTECTION_POLICY`, `SUBSCRIPTION_FILTER_POLICY`, `FIELD_INDEX_POLICY` or `TRANSFORMER_POLICY`. You can have one account policy per type in an account.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

scope.description: Currently defaults to and only accepts the value: `ALL`.

selection_criteria.description: - Criteria for applying a subscription filter policy to a selection of log groups. The only allowable criteria selector is `LogGroupName NOT IN []`.

anomaly_visibility_time.description: Number of days to have visibility on an anomaly. After this time period has elapsed for an anomaly, it will be automatically baselined and the anomaly detector will treat new occurrences of a similar anomaly as normal. Therefore, if you do not correct the cause of an anomaly during the time period specified in `anomaly_visibility_time`, it will be considered normal going forward and will not be detected as an anomaly. Valid Range: Minimum value of 7. Maximum value of 90.

evaluation_frequency.description: Specifies how often the anomaly detector is to run and look for anomalies. Set this value according to the frequency that the log group receives new logs. For example, if the log group receives new log events every 10 minutes, then 15 minutes might be a good setting for `evaluation_frequency`. Valid Values: `ONE_MIN | FIVE_MIN | TEN_MIN | FIFTEEN_MIN | THIRTY_MIN | ONE_HOUR`.

filter_pattern.description: You can use this parameter to limit the anomaly detection model to examine only log events that match the pattern you specify here. For more information, see [Filter and Pattern Syntax](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/FilterAndPatternSyntax.html).

kms_key_id.description: Optionally assigns a AWS KMS key to secure this anomaly detector and its findings. If a key is assigned, the anomalies found and the model used by this detector are encrypted at rest with the key. If a key is assigned to an anomaly detector, a user must have permissions for both this key and for the anomaly detector to retrieve information about the anomalies that it finds.

log_group_arn_list.description: Array containing the ARN of the log group that this anomaly detector will watch. You can specify only one log group ARN.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

log_group_name.description: The name of the log group under which the log stream is to be created.

policy_document.description: Specifies the data protection policy in JSON. Read more at [Data protection policy syntax](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/mask-sensitive-log-data-start.html#mask-sensitive-log-data-policysyntax).

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

delivery_destination_arn.description: The ARN of the delivery destination to use for this delivery.

delivery_source_name.description: The name of the delivery source to use for this delivery.

field_delimiter.description: The field delimiter to use between record fields when the final output format of a delivery is in `plain`, `w3c`, or `raw` format.

record_fields.description: The list of record fields to be delivered to the destination, in order.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

s3_delivery_configuration.description: Parameters that are valid only when the delivery's delivery destination is an S3 bucket.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

delivery_destination_configuration.description: The AWS resource that will receive the logs. Required for CloudWatch Logs, Amazon S3, and Firehose destinations. Not required for X-Ray trace delivery destinations.

delivery_destination_type.description: The type of delivery destination. Valid values: `S3`, `CWL`, `FH`, `XRAY`. Required for X-Ray trace delivery destinations. For other destination types, this is computed from the `destination_resource_arn`.

name.description: The name for this delivery destination.

output_format.description: The format of the logs that are sent to this delivery destination. Valid values: `json`, `plain`, `w3c`, `raw`, `parquet`.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

delivery_destination_name.description: The name of the delivery destination to assign this policy to.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

log_type.description: The type of log that the source is sending. For Amazon Bedrock, the valid value is `APPLICATION_LOGS`. For Amazon CodeWhisperer, the valid value is `EVENT_LOGS`. For IAM Identity Center, the valid value is `ERROR_LOGS`. For Amazon WorkMail, the valid values are `ACCESS_CONTROL_LOGS`, `AUTHENTICATION_LOGS`, `WORKMAIL_AVAILABILITY_PROVIDER_LOGS`, and `WORKMAIL_MAILBOX_ACCESS_LOGS`.

name.description: The name for this delivery source.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

resource_arn.description: The ARN of the AWS resource that is generating and sending logs.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

name.description: A name for the log destination.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

role_arn.description: The ARN of an IAM role that grants Amazon CloudWatch Logs permissions to put data into the target.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

target_arn.description: The ARN of the target Amazon Kinesis stream resource for the destination.

access_policy.description: The policy document. This is a JSON formatted string.

destination_name.description: A name for the subscription filter

force_update.description: Specify true if you are updating an existing destination policy to grant permission to an organization ID instead of granting permission to individual AWS accounts.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

kms_key_id.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data. Please note, after the AWS KMS CMK is disassociated from the log group,

log_group_class.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: `STANDARD`, `INFREQUENT_ACCESS`, or `DELIVERY`.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

skip_destroy.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

log_group_name.description: Log group name to set the policy for.

policy_document.description: JSON policy document. This is a JSON formatted string.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

apply_on_transformed_logs.description: Whether the metric filter will be applied on the transformed version of the log events instead of the original ingested log events. Defaults to `false`. Valid only for log groups that have an active log transformer.

log_group_name.description: The name of the log group to associate the metric filter with.

metric_transformation.description: A block defining collection of information needed to define how metric data gets emitted. See below.

metric_transformation.default_value.description: The value to emit when a filter pattern does not match a log event. Conflicts with `dimensions`.

metric_transformation.dimensions.description: Map of fields to use as dimensions for the metric. Up to 3 dimensions are allowed. Conflicts with `default_value`.

metric_transformation.name.description: The name of the CloudWatch metric to which the monitored log information should be published (e.g., `ErrorCount`)

metric_transformation.namespace.description: The destination namespace of the CloudWatch metric.

metric_transformation.unit.description: The unit to assign to the metric. If you omit this, the unit is set as `None`.

metric_transformation.value.description: What to publish to the metric. For example, if you're counting the occurrences of a particular term like "Error", the value will be "1" for each occurrence. If you're counting the bytes transferred the published value will be the value in the log event.

name.description: A name for the metric filter.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

policy_document.description: Details of the resource policy, including the identity of the principal that is enabled to put logs to this account. This is formatted as a JSON string. Maximum length of 5120 characters.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

log_group_name.description: The name of the log group under which the log stream is to be created.

name.description: The name of the log stream. Must not be longer than 512 characters and must not contain `:`

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

apply_on_transformed_logs.description: Boolean to indicate whether to apply the subscription filter on the transformed version of the log events instead of the original ingested log events. Defaults to `false`. Valid only for log groups that have an active log transformer.

destination_arn.description: ARN of the destination to deliver matching log events to. Kinesis stream or Lambda function ARN.

distribution.description: Method used to distribute log data to the destination. By default log data is grouped by log stream, but the grouping can be set to random for a more even distribution. This property is only applicable when the destination is an Amazon Kinesis stream. Valid values are "Random" and "ByLogStream".

emit_system_fields.description: List of system fields to include in the log events sent to the subscription destination. These fields provide source information for centralized log data in the forwarded payload. Valid values: `"@aws.account"`, `"@aws.region"`. To remove this argument after it has been set, specify an empty list `[]` explicitly to avoid perpetual differences.

filter_pattern.description: Valid CloudWatch Logs filter pattern for subscribing to a filtered stream of log events. Use empty string `""` to match everything. For more information, see the [Amazon CloudWatch Logs User Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/FilterAndPatternSyntax.html).

log_group_name.description: Name of the log group to associate the subscription filter with.

name.description: Name for the subscription filter.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

role_arn.description: ARN of an IAM role that grants CloudWatch Logs permissions to deliver ingested log events to the destination stream. You don't need to provide the ARN when you are working with a logical destination for cross-account delivery. If you use Lambda as a destination, you should skip this argument and use `aws_lambda_permission` resource for granting access from CloudWatch logs to the destination Lambda function.

add_keys.description: Adds new key-value pairs to the log event. See [`add_keys`](#add_keys-block) below for details.

add_keys.entry.description: Objects containing the information about the keys to add to the log event. You must include at least one entry, and five at most. See [`add_keys` `entry`](#add_keys-entry-block) below for details.

add_keys.key.description: Specifies the key of the new entry to be added to the log event.

add_keys.overwrite_if_exists.description: Specifies whether to overwrite the value if the key already exists in the log event. Defaults to `false`.

add_keys.value.description: Specifies the value of the new entry to be added to the log event.

copy_value.description: Copies values within a log event. See [`copy_value`](#copy_value-block) below for details.

copy_value.columns.description: Specifies the names to use for the columns in the transformed log event. If not specified, default column names (`[column_1, column_2 ...]`) are used.

copy_value.delimiter.description: Specifies the character used to separate each column in the original comma-separated value log event. Defaults to the comma `,` character.

copy_value.entry.description: Objects containing the information about the values to copy to the log event. You must include at least one entry, and five at most. See [`copy_value` `entry`](#copy_value-entry-block) below for details.

copy_value.locale.description: Specifies the locale of the source field. Defaults to `locale.ROOT`.

copy_value.match_patterns.description: Specifies the list of patterns to match against the `source` field.

copy_value.overwrite_if_exists.description: Specifies whether to overwrite the value if the destination key already exists. Defaults to `false`.

copy_value.quote_character.description: Specifies the character used as a text qualifier for a single column of data. Defaults to the double quotation mark `"` character.

copy_value.source.description: Specifies the key to apply the date conversion to.

copy_value.source_timezone.description: Specifies the time zone of the source field. Defaults to `UTC`.

copy_value.target.description: Specifies the JSON field to store the result in.

copy_value.target_format.description: Specifies the datetime format to use for the converted data in the target field. Defaults to `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z`.

copy_value.target_timezone.description: Specifies the time zone of the target field. Defaults to `UTC`.

copy_value.with_keys.description: Specifies the keys to be deleted.

csv.description: Parses comma-separated values (CSV) from the log events into columns. See [`csv`](#csv-block) below for details.

date_time_converter.description: Converts a datetime string into a format that you specify. See [`date_time_converter`](#date_time_converter-block) below for details.

delete_keys.description: Deletes entry from a log event. See [`delete_keys`](#delete_keys-block) below for details.

grok.description: Parses and structures unstructured data by using pattern matching. See [`grok`](#grok-block) below for details.

grok.entry.description: Objects containing the information about the keys to move to the log event. You must include at least one entry, and five at most. See [`move_keys` `entry`](#move_keys-entry-block) below for details.

grok.flatten.description: Specifies whether the list will be flattened into single items. Defaults to `false`.

grok.flattened_element.description: Required if `flatten` is set to true. Specifies the element to keep. Allowed values are `first` and `last`.

grok.key.description: Specifies the key of the field to be extracted as keys in the generated map.

grok.match.description: Specifies the grok pattern to match against the log event.

grok.overwrite_if_exists.description: Specifies whether to overwrite the value if the destination key already exists. Defaults to `false`.

grok.target.description: Specifies the key to move to.

grok.value_key.description: Specifies the values that will be extracted from the source objects and put into the values of the generated map. If omitted, original objects in the source list will be put into the values of the generated map.

grok.with_keys.description: Specifies the keys of the fields to convert to lowercase.

list_to_map.description: Converts list of objects that contain key fields into a map of target keys. See [`list_to_map`](#list_to_map-block) below for details.

log_group_arn.description: Log group ARN to set the transformer for.

lower_case_string.description: Converts a string to lowercase. See [`lower_case_string`](#lower_case_string-block) below for details.

move_keys.description: Moves a key from one field to another. See [`move_keys`](#move_keys-block) below for details.

parse_cloudfront.description: Parses CloudFront vended logs, extracts fields, and converts them into JSON format. See [`parse_cloudfront`](#parse_cloudfront-block) below for details.

parse_cloudfront.destination.description: Specifies the destination field to put the extracted key-value pairs into.

parse_cloudfront.field_delimiter.description: Specifies the field delimiter string that is used between key-value pairs in the original log events. Defaults to the ampersand `&` character.

parse_cloudfront.key_prefix.description: Specifies a prefix that will be added to all transformed keys.

parse_cloudfront.key_value_delimiter.description: Specifies the delimiter string to use between the key and value in each pair in the transformed log event. Defaults to the equal `=` character.

parse_cloudfront.non_match_value.description: Specifies a value to insert into the value field in the result if a key-value pair is not successfully split.

parse_cloudfront.overwrite_if_exists.description: Specifies whether to overwrite the value if the destination key already exists. Defaults to `false`.

parse_cloudfront.source.description: Specifies the path to the field in the log event that will be parsed. Defaults to `@message`.

parse_json.description: Parses log events that are in JSON format. See [`parse_json`](#parse_json-block) below for details.

parse_key_value.description: Parses a specified field in the original log event into key-value pairs. See [`parse_key_value`](#parse_key_value-block) below for details.

parse_postgres.description: Parses RDS for PostgreSQL vended logs, extracts fields, and and convert them into a JSON format. See [`parse_postgres`](#parse_postgres-block) below for details.

parse_postgres.source.description: Specifies the source field to be parsed. The only allowed value is `@message`. If omitted, the whole log message is processed.

parse_route53.description: Parses Route 53 vended logs, extracts fields, and converts them into JSON format. See [`parse_route53`](#parse_route53-block) below for details.

parse_route53.source.description: Specifies the source field to be parsed. The only allowed value is `@message`. If omitted, the whole log message is processed.

parse_to_ocsf.description: Parses logs events and converts them into Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework (OCSF) events. See [`parse_to_ocsf`](#parse_to_ocsf-block) below for details.

parse_to_ocsf.event_type.description: Specifies the service or process that produces the log events. Allowed values are: `CloudTrail`, `Route53Resolver`, `VPCFlow`, `EKSAudit`, and `AWSWAF`.

parse_to_ocsf.ocsf_version.description: Specifies the version of the OCSF schema to use for the transformed log events. The only allowed value is `V1.1`.

parse_to_ocsf.source.description: Specifies the source field to be parsed. The only allowed value is `@message`. If omitted, the whole log message is processed.

parse_vpc.description: Parses Amazon VPC vended logs, extracts fields, and converts them into JSON format. See [`parse_vpc`](#parse_vpc-block) below for details.

parse_vpc.source.description: Specifies the source field to be parsed. The only allowed value is `@message`. If omitted, the whole log message is processed.

parse_waf.description: Parses AWS WAF vended logs, extracts fields, and converts them into JSON format. See [`parse_waf`](#parse_waf-block) below for details.

parse_waf.delimiter.description: Specifies the separator characters to split the string entry on.

parse_waf.entry.description: Objects containing the information about the fields to change the type of. You must include at least one entry, and five at most. See [`type_converter` `entry`](#type_converter-entry-block) below for details.

parse_waf.from.description: Specifies the regular expression string to be replaced.

parse_waf.key.description: Specifies the key with the value that will be converted to a different type.

parse_waf.overwrite_if_exists.description: Specifies whether to overwrite the value if the destination key already exists. Defaults to `false`.

parse_waf.renameTo.description: Specifies the new name of the key.

parse_waf.to.description: Specifies the string to be substituted for each match of `from`.

parse_waf.type.description: Specifies the type to convert the field value to. Allowed values are: `integer`, `double`, `string` and `boolean`.

parse_waf.with_keys.description: Specifies the keys of the fields to convert to uppercase.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

rename_keys.description: Renames keys in a log event. See [`rename_keys`](#rename_keys-block) below for details.

split_string.description: Splits a field into an array of strings using a delimiting character. See [`split_string`](#split_string-block) below for details.

substitute_string.description: Matches a key’s value against a regular expression and replaces all matches with a replacement string. See [`substitute_string`](#substitute_string-block) below for details.

transformer_config.description: Specifies the configuration of the transformer. You must include at least one configuration, and 20 at most. See [`transformer_config`](#transformer_config-block) below for details.

trim_string.description: Removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string. See [`trim_string`](#trim_string-block) below for details.

type_converter.description: Converts a value type associated with the specified key to the specified type. See [`type_converter`](#type_converter-block) below for details.

upper_case_string.description: Converts a string to uppercase. See [`upper_case_string`](#upper_case_string-block) below for details.

account_id.description: The ID of the account where the metrics are located, if this is a cross-account alarm.

actions_enabled.description: Indicates whether or not actions should be executed during any changes to the alarm's state. Defaults to `true`.

alarm_actions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an ALARM state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

alarm_name.description: The descriptive name for the alarm. This name must be unique within the user's AWS account

comparison_operator.description: The arithmetic operation to use when comparing the specified Statistic and Threshold. The specified Statistic value is used as the first operand. Either of the following is supported: `GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold`, `GreaterThanThreshold`, `LessThanThreshold`, `LessThanOrEqualToThreshold`. Additionally, the values  `LessThanLowerOrGreaterThanUpperThreshold`, `LessThanLowerThreshold`, and `GreaterThanUpperThreshold` are used only for alarms based on anomaly detection models.

datapoints_to_alarm.description: The number of data points that must be breaching to trigger the alarm.

dimensions.description: The dimensions for this metric.  For the list of available dimensions see the AWS documentation [here](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/DeveloperGuide/CW_Support_For_AWS.html).

evaluate_low_sample_count_percentiles.description: Used only for alarms based on percentiles.

evaluation_periods.description: The number of periods over which data is compared to the specified threshold.

expression.description: A Metrics Insights query or a metric math expression to be evaluated on the returned data.

extended_statistic.description: The percentile statistic for the metric associated with the alarm. Specify a value between p0.0 and p100.

id.description: A short name used to tie this object to the results in the response. If you are performing math expressions on this set of data, this name represents that data and can serve as a variable in the mathematical expression. The valid characters are letters, numbers, and underscore. The first character must be a lowercase letter.

insufficient_data_actions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an INSUFFICIENT_DATA state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

label.description: A human-readable label for this metric or expression. This is especially useful if this is an expression, so that you know what the value represents.

metric.description: The metric to be returned, along with statistics, period, and units. Use this parameter only if this object is retrieving a metric and not performing a math expression on returned data.

namespace.description: The namespace for this metric. See docs for the [list of namespaces](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/DeveloperGuide/aws-namespaces.html).

ok_actions.description: The list of actions to execute when this alarm transitions into an OK state from any other state. Each action is specified as an Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

period.description: Granularity in seconds of returned data points.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

return_data.description: Specify exactly one `metric_query` to be `true` to use that `metric_query` result as the alarm.

stat.description: The statistic to apply to this metric.

statistic.description: The statistic to apply to the alarm's associated metric.

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

threshold.description: The value against which the specified statistic is compared. This parameter is required for alarms based on static thresholds, but should not be used for alarms based on anomaly detection models.

threshold_metric_id.description: If this is an alarm based on an anomaly detection model, make this value match the ID of the ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND function.

treat_missing_data.description: Sets how this alarm is to handle missing data points. The following values are supported: `missing`, `ignore`, `breaching` and `notBreaching`. Defaults to `missing`.

additional_statistics.description: The additional statistics to stream for the metrics listed in `include_metrics`.

exclude_filter.description: List of exclusive metric filters. If you specify this parameter, the stream sends metrics from all metric namespaces except for the namespaces and the conditional metric names that you specify here. If you don't specify metric names or provide empty metric names whole metric namespace is excluded. Conflicts with `include_filter`.

firehose_arn.description: ARN of the Amazon Kinesis Firehose delivery stream to use for this metric stream.

include_filter.description: List of inclusive metric filters. If you specify this parameter, the stream sends only the conditional metric names from the metric namespaces that you specify here. If you don't specify metric names or provide empty metric names whole metric namespace is included. Conflicts with `exclude_filter`.

include_metric.description: An array that defines the metrics that are to have additional statistics streamed. See details below.

metric_names.description: An array that defines the metrics you want to include for this metric namespace

output_format.description: Output format for the stream. Possible values are `json`, `opentelemetry0.7`, and `opentelemetry1.0`. For more information about output formats, see [Metric streams output formats](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/CloudWatch-metric-streams-formats.html).

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

role_arn.description: ARN of the IAM role that this metric stream will use to access Amazon Kinesis Firehose resources. For more information about role permissions, see [Trust between CloudWatch and Kinesis Data Firehose](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/CloudWatch-metric-streams-trustpolicy.html).

statistics_configuration.description: For each entry in this array, you specify one or more metrics and the list of additional statistics to stream for those metrics. The additional statistics that you can stream depend on the stream's `output_format`. If the OutputFormat is `json`, you can stream any additional statistic that is supported by CloudWatch, listed in [CloudWatch statistics definitions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/Statistics-definitions.html.html). If the OutputFormat is `opentelemetry0.7` or `opentelemetry1.0`, you can stream percentile statistics (p99 etc.). See details below.

tags.description: Map of tags to assign to the resource. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

log_group_names.description: Specific log groups to use with the query.

name.description: The name of the query.

query_string.description: The query to save. You can read more about CloudWatch Logs Query Syntax in the [documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/CWL_QuerySyntax.html).

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

domain.description: The name of the domain to create. All domain names in an AWS Region that are in the same AWS account must be unique. The domain name is used as the prefix in DNS hostnames. Do not use sensitive information in a domain name because it is publicly discoverable.

encryption_key.description: The encryption key for the domain. This is used to encrypt content stored in a domain. The KMS Key Amazon Resource Name (ARN). The default aws/codeartifact AWS KMS master key is used if this element is absent.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

tags.description: Key-value map of resource tags. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

domain.description: The name of the domain on which to set the resource policy.

domain_owner.description: The account number of the AWS account that owns the domain.

policy_document.description: A JSON policy string to be set as the access control resource policy on the provided domain.

policy_revision.description: The current revision of the resource policy to be set. This revision is used for optimistic locking, which prevents others from overwriting your changes to the domain's resource policy.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

description.description: The description of the repository.

domain.description: The domain that contains the created repository.

domain_owner.description: The account number of the AWS account that owns the domain.

external_connection_name.description: The name of the external connection associated with a repository.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

repository.description: The name of the repository to create.

repository_name.description: The name of an upstream repository.

tags.description: Key-value map of resource tags. If configured with a provider [`default_tags` configuration block](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#default_tags-configuration-block) present, tags with matching keys will overwrite those defined at the provider-level.

upstream.description: A list of upstream repositories to associate with the repository. The order of the upstream repositories in the list determines their priority order when AWS CodeArtifact looks for a requested package version. see [Upstream](#upstream)

domain.description: The name of the domain on which to set the resource policy.

domain_owner.description: The account number of the AWS account that owns the domain.

policy_document.description: A JSON policy string to be set as the access control resource policy on the provided domain.

policy_revision.description: The current revision of the resource policy to be set. This revision is used for optimistic locking, which prevents others from overwriting your changes to the domain's resource policy.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

repository.description: The name of the repository to set the resource policy on.

base_capacity.description: Number of machines allocated to the ﬂeet.

compute_configuration.description: The compute configuration of the compute fleet. This is only required if `compute_type` is set to `ATTRIBUTE_BASED_COMPUTE` or `CUSTOM_INSTANCE_TYPE`. See [`compute_configuration`](#compute_configuration) below.

compute_type.description: Compute resources the compute fleet uses. See [compute types](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codebuild/latest/userguide/build-env-ref-compute-types.html#environment.types) for more information and valid values.

disk.description: Amount of disk space of the instance type included in the fleet.

environment_type.description: Environment type of the compute fleet. See [environment types](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codebuild/latest/userguide/build-env-ref-compute-types.html#environment.types) for more information and valid values.

minimum_healthy_hosts.description: A minimum_healthy_hosts block. Required for `Server` compute platform. Minimum Healthy Hosts are documented below.

minimum_healthy_hosts.type.description: The type can either be `FLEET_PERCENT` or `HOST_COUNT`.

minimum_healthy_hosts.value.description: The value when the type is `FLEET_PERCENT` represents the minimum number of healthy instances as

minimum_healthy_hosts_per_zone.type.description: The type can either be `FLEET_PERCENT` or `HOST_COUNT`.

minimum_healthy_hosts_per_zone.value.description: The value when the type is `FLEET_PERCENT` represents the minimum number of healthy instances as a percentage of the total number of instances in the Availability Zone during a deployment. If you specify FLEET_PERCENT, at the start of the deployment, AWS CodeDeploy converts the percentage to the equivalent number of instance and rounds up fractional instances. When the type is `HOST_COUNT`, the value represents the minimum number of healthy instances in the Availability Zone as an absolute value.

region.description: Region where this resource will be [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#regional-endpoints). Defaults to the Region set in the [provider configuration](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs#aws-configuration-reference).

time_based_canary.interval.description: The number of minutes between the first and second traffic shifts of a `TimeBasedCanary` deployment.

time_based_canary.percentage.description: The percentage of traffic to shift in the first increment of a `TimeBasedCanary` deployment.

time_based_linear.interval.description: The number of minutes between each incremental traffic shift of a `TimeBasedLinear` deployment.

time_based_linear.percentage.description: The percentage of traffic that is shifted at the start of each increment of a `TimeBasedLinear` deployment.

traffic_routing_config.description: A traffic_routing_config block. Traffic Routing Config is documented below.

traffic_routing_config.time_based_canary.description: The time based canary configuration information. If `type` is `TimeBasedLinear`, use `time_based_linear` instead.

traffic_routing_config.time_based_linear.description: The time based linear configuration information. If `type` is `TimeBasedCanary`, use `time_based_canary` instead.

traffic_routing_config.type.description: Type of traffic routing config. One of `TimeBasedCanary`, `TimeBasedLinear`, `AllAtOnce`.

zonal_config.description: A zonal_config block. Zonal Config is documented below.

zonal_config.first_zone_monitor_duration.description: The period of time, in seconds, that CodeDeploy must wait after completing a deployment to the first Availability Zone. CodeDeploy will wait this amount of time before starting a deployment to the second Availability Zone. If you don't specify a value for `first_zone_monitor_duration_in_seconds`, then CodeDeploy uses the `monitor_duration_in_seconds` value for the first Availability Zone.

zonal_config.minimum_healthy_hosts_per_z.description: The number or percentage of instances that must remain available per Availability Zone during a deployment. If you don't specify a value under `minimum_healthy_hosts_per_zone`, then CodeDeploy uses a default value of 0 percent. This block is more documented below.

zonal_config.monitor_duration_in_seconds.description: The period of time, in seconds, that CodeDeploy must wait after completing a deployment to an Availability Zone. CodeDeploy will wait this amount of time before starting a deployment to the next Availability Zone. If you don't specify a `monitor_duration_in_seconds`, CodeDeploy starts deploying to the next Availability Zone immediately.

action.description: The action to take on instances in the original environment after a successful blue/green deployment.

action_on_timeout.description: When to reroute traffic from an original environment to a replacement environment in a blue/green deployment.

alarm_configuration.description: Configuration block of alarms associated with the deployment group (documented below).

alarms.description: A list of alarms configured for the deployment group.

auto_rollback_configuration.description: Configuration block of the automatic rollback configuration associated with the deployment group (documented below).

autoscaling_groups.description: Autoscaling groups associated with the deployment group.
